message:
fix(mcp/wa.mission_state): return MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS when mission_state filter misses (ft-c1lfg)

Before: in WaMissionStateTool at crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:3697-3725, when the caller
passed params.mission_state and the active mission's lifecycle_state
did not match it, the tool still returned a SUCCESS envelope — just
with every counter zeroed out and an empty assignments vec. A client
could not distinguish "no such mission" from "filter missed" from
"mission is genuinely empty" without cross-checking the mission_hash
or lifecycle_state fields, and the failure was silent by default
(`ok: true`, `data: {...}`).

The documented behavior (and the invariant other wa.mission_* tools
enforce) is that a filter mismatch is an argument-level error, not a
success with a synthetic empty payload. This was filed against the
MCP contract rather than as a cosmetic cleanup because MCP clients
that treat `ok` as a fast-path branch were silently skipping the
mismatch case on every poll.

Fix: on filter miss, return:

    McpEnvelope::<()>::error(
        MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS,
        "mission_state filter '{requested}' did not match active mission lifecycle_state '{current}'",
        Some("Use wa.mission_state without mission_state to inspect the active mission, or request the current lifecycle state."),
        elapsed_ms(start),
    )

so clients get a deterministic error_code + hint path and can branch
cleanly. The full McpMissionStateData constructor is dropped from
this branch (no McpMissionAssignmentCounters allocation on the
miss path), and the per-tool envelope shape stays consistent with
what the tests in mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs pin.

Test: mission_state_tool_rejects_mission_state_filter_miss asserts
the exact error_code (MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS), the verbatim error
message, the verbatim hint string, and data is absent. Backed by a
sample_mission(state) + write_mission_file(dir, state) helper pair
added alongside the existing test module so the lifecycle_state is
varied through a real Mission instance (planner/dispatcher/operator
ownership, one Assignment, one CandidateAction, one ReservationIntent,
one Outcome::Success) rather than a hand-rolled JSON blob.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
